description_long: This is a hard-sided, rectangular briefcase with a rugged, metallic exterior and a textured, foam-lined interior. The case is open, revealing two green C4 explosive blocks labeled 'C4 EXPLOSIVE' and a digital keypad device connected by colored wires. The foam padding inside is egg-crate style, providing cushioning and secure placement for the contents. The exterior shows signs of wear, with visible scuffs and a matte, slightly weathered finish. The case features a single, sturdy handle and a simple latch mechanism for closure, but no visible brand markings or external pockets. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing protection and secure transport of sensitive or hazardous contents.
description: A rugged, metallic briefcase with a foam-lined interior, containing C4 explosives and a digital keypad device. The exterior is worn and matte, with a single handle and no visible branding or external pockets.
description_view_0: The open briefcase reveals two green C4 explosive blocks and a digital keypad device set into the foam-lined interior.
description_view_1: Side view of the open case, highlighting the thickness of the foam padding and the arrangement of the explosives and keypad.
description_view_2: Rear view of the closed exterior, showing the worn, metallic surface with no visible features or markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of the open case, again showing the foam interior and the placement of the C4 and keypad from a different angle.
attribute_handle_type: Single fixed rectangular handle, likely metal or hard plastic, attached with metal brackets to the front edge of the case.
attribute_lock_mechanism: Simple latch mechanism at the front; no visible combination or key lock.
